blocks: You hugged me.
Then let go.

Then hugged me
one more time.

I don't think
either of us

was ready
for that second goodbye.

I don't remember
how many games we played.

I only remember
looking up
and finding you
on the other side of the table,
completely determined
to win.

The laughter
was louder
than the puck.

When it ended,
the scoreboard read
7–4.

Somehow,
it became
my favourite game
to lose.

Somehow,
that little balcony
became our dining table.

Maggi.
Pasta.
Whatever we felt like eating.
It never really mattered.

What I remember most
is reaching across
to feed you
with my hands.

Looking back now,
I think that was the moment
taking care of you
stopped feeling like something I did...

and simply became
the most natural thing
in the world.

The perfume.
The handwritten diary.
The bouquet.
The cake.
The balcony.

They're all memories
I'll hold onto forever.

But if anyone ever asked me
what the best birthday gift
I've ever received was

My answer
would always be the same.
You.

You travelled across cities
just so I wouldn't wake up alone
on my birthday.

I don't think
I'll ever find words
big enough
to thank you for that♥️
Happy Three Months, Bebu.

People say
home is a place.

You quietly proved
it can be a person.

Thank you
for flying all the way
just to celebrate my birthday
and turning it into
one of the happiest weekends
of my life.

Thank you
for every laugh.
Every hug.
Every kiss.
Every cup of coffee.
Every little moment
that somehow became
a memory I'll carry forever.

Thank you
for making ordinary days
feel like the kind
I'll spend a lifetime
looking back on.

If someone ever asked me
where my favourite place
in the world is,

I don't think
I'd name a city anymore.
I'd simply say,
"Anywhere you're standing."

I love you.
Always♥️
— Harisikh

You looked at me
and smiled.
Then you said,
"Mereko toh Green Forest mila hai."
I smiled back.

I didn't ask
what it meant.

The truth is,
I still don't know
exactly what you meant.

I only know
I've never wanted
to be
anything else.

I don't even remember
what the episode
was about.

I only remember
Shinchan looking up
“Aaj toh Kusurimas hai."

And somehow...
we couldn't stop laughing.

It wasn't clever.
It didn't make sense.
It was just ours.
To my favourite person,

I don't know how many times
I started making this.

Or how many versions
quietly disappeared
because they never felt enough.

Every time I thought
I'd finally found the right way
to preserve those three days,
I'd look at it
and realise it only showed
what happened.
Not what it felt like.

Photographs remember faces.
Videos remember voices.

But neither of them remembers
the million tiny things
that happened inside my head
while we were living those moments.

This isn't a website.
It isn't a timeline.
And it definitely isn't
every memory we made.

It's simply the closest thing
I could build
to letting you step back
into those three days.
Not to remember them.
But to quietly live them again.

Some of these moments are big.
Most of them aren't.

The strange thing is,

The ones that changed me the most
were never the dramatic ones.

They were the ordinary moments
that somehow refused
to stay ordinary.

You'll probably smile at some.
You'll probably laugh at others.

A few of them
might seem so small
that anyone else
would have forgotten them.

I'm just glad
we didn't.
So,
Come back with me.
Let's relive those three days.
Bebu
Pull the photograph out.

Bebu,
Happy 3 Months. ♥️

If you've reached this page,
then you've walked through
every little memory
I wanted to keep with us.

Some of them were big.
Most of them weren't.

But somehow,
every one of them
became important
simply because you were there.

You're the best person
I have ever met.
Not because you're perfect.

But because you make
ordinary days
feel like the ones
I'll remember forever.

Thank you
for every laugh,
every hug,
every "Kya hua, Bebu?",
every quiet moment,
and every memory
that found its way
into these pages.

If there's one thing
I hope you carry with you
after closing this notebook,
it's this:

You are deeply,
endlessly,
and completely loved.

And if life gave me
another chance
to begin this story...
I'd still choose you.
Every single time.

Happy 3 Months,
my favourite person.
I love you. ♥️

There are airports
all over the world.

Thousands of people
walk through those same
sliding doors
every single day.

But I don't think
I'll ever look at an airport
the same way again.

I reached
much earlier
than I needed to.

Walked one circle.
Checked the arrivals screen.
Checked my phone.
Looked at the exit again.

Then repeated
the whole thing.

I kept pretending
I wasn't nervous.

As if arriving early
somehow made me look calmer.
It didn't.

The doors kept opening.
People kept coming out.

Families.
Couples.
Business travellers.

Everyone,
except the person
I was waiting for.
And then,
There you were.

Holding your suitcase.
Looking around
until our eyes met.

I don't really remember
the airport after that.

I only remember
you.

I don't think
you ever realised
what you actually gave me
that night.

People celebrate birthdays
all the time.

They buy cakes.
They buy gifts.
They take photographs.

But that's not
what I remember.

I remember
opening the door,

and realising
every single thing
I was looking at
existed for one reason.

You wanted
to see me smile.

I don't think
I've ever received
a more thoughtful birthday
than that.

I still smile
every time I see this.

Whenever you're this close,
I always end up
kissing you.

I don't even realise
I'm doing it anymore.
It just happens.

As naturally
as reaching for your hand
or smiling back at you.

I kept pretending
there was still
a little more time.

One more conversation.
One more hug.
One more minute.

As if
not looking at the clock
could somehow stop it
from moving.
It couldn't.

And somehow,
the shortest walk
of those three days
became the hardest one.

Not because
I didn't understand
why you had to leave.

But because
understanding it
didn't make it
any easier.

You probably thought
I was giving you flowers.

But that wasn't really
what I was doing.

Those lilies
were my way of saying,
"I'm really glad you're here."

without having to say
anything at all.

I'd imagined
that moment
so many times
before you came.

Where I'd stand.
Whether I'd smile.
Whether I'd look awkward.
Whether I'd forget
everything I wanted to say.
Turns out,

Reality was
much simpler.

I saw you.
You smiled.

And suddenly,
none of it mattered anymore.
Because somehow,

You made
everything
feel natural

I don't remember
a single road
we took that evening.

I couldn't tell you
which traffic signal was red.

Or where we slowed down.
Or how many turns we made.

I only remember
one thing.
At some point,

without either of us
saying anything,

you wrapped your arms
around me.
So naturally.

As if they'd always
belonged there.
I didn't realise it then.
I realised it later.

holding on
quietly stopped meaning
the scooty.

It had become
me.

That's the strange thing
about beautiful moments.

They almost never
announce themselves.
They just happen.
And months later,

you suddenly find yourself
wishing
you could go back
for five more minutes

I didn't realise it then.
I only realised it later.

People fall asleep
when they're tired.

But they only fall asleep
like this,
when they feel safe.

I wasn't trying
to rest.

I wasn't trying
to make a memory.
It just happened.

Looking back now,
I think
that's my favourite part
of those three days.

Not because
anything happened.

But because
nothing needed to

That pink nightsuit of yours
is still unfairly cute.

I don't think
you'll ever convince me otherwise.
You always
started it.
A poke.
A smile.
Then suddenly,
I couldn't stop laughing.
I'd try
to tickle you back,
but somehow,
you always won.
Then you'd look at me
trying to catch my breath
and ask,
"Kya hua, bebu?"
White Rabbit.
I smiled the second I found it.
Because of course you hid one.

Even in a room
you had already filled
with love,
you still wanted me
to find
one more reason
to smile.
